strategy: Agent-Scanner: Quality Analyzer evolves from static text analysis into an agent with tools (read_file, list_dir, grep, compare). This decouples QA from format — works on any toolkit, any agent, any structure. Wireframe (P-MAS-architector v3) = end-state spec, NOT a separate project. AAA-studio IS the wireframe, built in phases.

tasks.title: Add Quality Analyzer page to in-app Wiki

roadmap.name: Agent-Scanner — QA becomes an agent with tools
roadmap.vision: Quality Analyzer evolves from static text analysis into an agent with tools (read_file, list_dir, grep, compare, exec). This decouples QA from input format — works on any toolkit, any agent, any structure. Not format-dependent, but context-aware.

tasks.title: Smoke test: run current QA on zai-agent-toolkit (110+ skills)
tasks.notes: Clone toolkit, feed to QA via ZIP/GitHub, see what breaks. Real data > roadmap fantasy.

tasks.title: Define agent tools for scanner (read_file, list_dir, grep, compare)
tasks.notes: Tool definitions for the scanning agent. Based on 4.1 findings.

tasks.title: Build scanner agent — flow with tools in Flow Editor
tasks.notes: Flow: Input -> read_file x N -> grep patterns -> LLM analysis -> output. Executed via existing Flow Engine.

tasks.title: Wire scanner flow into Quality Analyzer UI
tasks.notes: QA page triggers scanner flow instead of /api/evaluate-deep. Shows results in existing panels.

tasks.title: Add cross-reference checking (files reference each other)
tasks.notes: Agent compares: skill mentions standard X -> does standard X exist? -> is it registered?

tasks.title: Re-test on zai-agent-toolkit — compare static QA vs agent-scanner
tasks.notes: Side-by-side: current QA score vs agent-scanner score on same toolkit. Data drives next steps.

roadmap.name: SonarMAS — 9-Metric Quality Engine (wireframe Tab Quality)
roadmap.notes: Depends on Epic 4 findings. Data from 4.6 determines what to build. From P-MAS wireframe: 9 metrics, A-F grade, radar chart.

tasks.title: Implement 9-metric weighted scoring engine
tasks.notes: Skill Completeness, Standard Coverage, Cross-Ref Integrity, Anti-Pattern Score, Version Consistency, Doc Freshness, Test Coverage, Registry Alignment, Conflict Resolution

tasks.title: Add Grade A-F badge + Radar chart
Implement 7 anti-pattern detectors (from wireframe Tab Issues)
Skill Classification (5 axes) + Standard Classification (4 types)

keyDecisions.title: No separate wireframe build
keyDecisions.decision: P-MAS-architector wireframe = target spec for AAA-studio QA, NOT a separate project. Build in phases inside AAA-studio.

keyDecisions.title: QA becomes agent with tools
keyDecisions.decision: Instead of hardcoding parsers per format, QA evolves into an agent that uses tools (read_file, grep, compare). Format-agnostic.

keyDecisions.title: Real data first, roadmap second
keyDecisions.decision: Test on zai-agent-toolkit (110+ skills) BEFORE building Epic 5. Data drives what metrics/detectors to implement.

qaTestResults.pattern: Examples consistently lowest (4-6/10). All other dimensions 9+. Static text analysis works but limited to format-dependent parsing.
qaTestResults.conclusion: Next step: agent-scanner approach (Epic 4) to decouple from format. Test on real toolkit (110+ skills) before building more metrics.

gates.epicGate: npx tsc --noEmit && bun run build && bunx verify-docs --ci
